Getting started

Install the npm package, set up a repo wiki, generate the site, and verify the first local output.

Home / Articles
ArticleFor users getting-startedinstructionshomepage

Summary

Install the npm package, set up a repo wiki, generate the site, and verify the first local output.

Article

Install Wikiwiki in the repo that should own the wiki, then run setup from that repo root:

test "$(npm prefix)" = "$PWD" || npm init -y

npm install --prefix "$PWD" --save-dev @thjodann/wk

npx wk setup --profile mixed --audience all

After setup, generate the human-facing output with wk validate, wk render, and wk site --audience all. If the repo wants a branded static site, run wk theme preview --json first, then wk theme init once the preview looks right.

The first successful verification is simple: open wiki-site/index.html and confirm the generated site loads, search works, and source/wiki links resolve.
